title: Robert Parish: Talk of Celtics trading Jaylen Brown is ‘disturbing’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics legend Robert Parish criticizes the idea of trading Jaylen Brown, calling it a serious miscalculation.
Despite a failed trade with Milwaukee, Boston continues to entertain offers for Jaylen Brown post-playoff exit.
Celtics' asking price for Jaylen Brown may need adjustment due to his reportedly decreased market value.

title: State Police sergeant, former lieutenant under criminal investigation for alleged coverup in wake of fatal crash, court documents reveal
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts State Police Sgt. Mark Delaney and former Lt. Anthony DeLucia are under criminal investigation for alleged misconduct in a fatal crash case.
Sgt. Scott Quigley was indicted for motor vehicle homicide after driving intoxicated in a 2023 crash that killed Angelo Schettino.
Quigley's indictment delayed the Phan brothers' murder trial, where he was scheduled as a witness, linking the cases via potential conduct issues.

title: 5 players Bruins should target in free agency on Wednesday
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins have $7.7 million in cap space for potential free agency signings starting Wednesday.
Defensive options considered by the Bruins include Ryan Shea, Jacob Trouba, and Nick Blankenburg for strengthening their blue line.
Offensive prospects like Matias Maccelli and Mason Marchment are potential signings to add scoring depth to the Bruins' roster.

title: Watch: Red Sox, Nationals brawl after Willson Contreras charges mound
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox's Willson Contreras charged Nationals' Cade Cavalli after an exchange of words following a strikeout at Fenway Park.
The brawl between the Red Sox and Nationals led to three ejections: Contreras, Miles Mikolas, and Nate Eaton.
Contreras, dealing with emotional stress due to Venezuelan earthquakes, had been ejected Monday for mocking an umpire's call.

title: Person shot and killed, suspect also dead after incident that closed I-495 in Lowell for hours, officials say
author: Darin Zullo and Samantha Genzer
bullets: A shooting led to the closure of I-495 in Lowell; one person was killed by gunfire.
The suspect in the shooting died by self-inflicted gunshot after a standoff on I-495.
Traffic on I-495 was diverted for hours; all lanes reopened by 10 p.m.

title: Dunkin’ drops new ‘Eagle Cup’ for America’s 250th
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dunkin' released a limited-edition 'Eagle Cup' for $10.99 to mark America's 250th anniversary.
Each 'Eagle Cup' comes with a free medium beverage and a 30-day discount on medium drinks.
The reusable cup is available nationwide while supplies last, with a limit of 20 per store.

title: NBA insider: Celtics must ‘significantly’ lower asking price in Jaylen Brown trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics reportedly asking for up to four first-round picks and players to trade Jaylen Brown.
Jaylen Brown is frustrated with trade rumors after a strong season leading the Celtics to 56 wins.
Portland Trail Blazers' interest in Brown is moderate; other teams like the Nuggets lack assets to meet Celtics' demands.

title: 3 injured in Theater District shooting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Three men were injured in a shooting on Tremont Street in Boston's Theater District Monday night.
Boston Police reported that all three victims suffered non-life-threatening injuries and were hospitalized.
No arrests have been made, and authorities are seeking information from the public for their investigation.

title: Healey vows urgent response as Haverhill sewage spill fouls Merrimack River
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Governor Maura Healey coordinates response after Haverhill sewage spill sends millions of gallons into Merrimack River.
Beaches in Ipswich, Newburyport, Essex, and Salisbury closed due to water contamination from 8 million gallons daily discharge.
Massachusetts agencies conducting water quality tests; shellfish areas temporarily closed as a precaution in affected locations.

title: Bruins ‘comfortable’ taking a leap of faith on two goalie prospects in 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins selected goalies Yuri Ivanov and Roberto Henriquez in the 2026 NHL Draft to bolster depth.
Ivanov was picked 56th overall and impressed with MHK Spartak Moskva, showcasing athleticism and skill.
Henriquez, joining Boston College in the fall, was praised for his competitiveness with the USHL's Green Bay Gamblers.
